# ID-25: Roadmap §3 restructure — phases become top-level Tasks

Per s48-feedback.md general comments on the TM schema reference, restructure `docs/reference/product-roadmap.json` §3 such that each phase becomes its own top-level Task ID. Currently the roadmap §3 carries phase-level work in a JSON shape that does not map cleanly to the TM Task/Subtask hierarchy.

The goal: a single uniform shape across roadmap + backlog + task-list, all using the TM Task/Subtask schema with `details` + `testStrategy` + `subtasks` + `cross_doc_links` fields. This unblocks the eventual Linear migration (deferred per §9.3) and aligns the three ledgers operationally.

## Acceptance criteria

- `product-roadmap.json` §3 phases re-shaped as top-level Tasks (one per phase), each with Subtask decomposition where applicable.
- `validateRoadmap` (or equivalent Zod schema) passes.
- Cross-doc links between roadmap Tasks and corresponding task-list Tasks or backlog items maintained.
- roadmap_schema-roundtrip test (analogous to backlog-schema-roundtrip ID-15.12) added for CI regression catch.

## Dependencies

ID-15 complete (Phase B schema alignment already shipped roadmap shape baseline); cannot start until ID-15.10 promote convention is internalised (done).

## Cancellation note (S62 W2)

**SUPERSEDED by ID-30 (consolidation + roadmap rethink) + ID-31 (canonical-pipeline migration approach).** Per Liam ratification at S62 W2 session-start: the §3 phase-restructure approach was premised on the inverted roadmap/backlog semantic (which the S60 ID-15.9 audit verified as CLEAN under wrong semantic per Branch B target-rules). User intent is Linear-style: consolidate roadmap content to backlog (post canonical-pipeline collapse) + rethink roadmap as a thin capability-themes surface (Shape A per ID-30 §4 research). Phase-restructure is no longer the right shape. Status: pending → cancelled.

<info added on 2026-05-24T12:00:00.000Z>
kh-main-S261 W1 schema-correctness fix: the 4 subtasks of this CANCELLED Task carried status `cancelled`, which SubtaskStatus deliberately excludes as a Task-level-only value (lib/validation/task-list-schema.ts:44) — making the canonical ledger unparseable by parseTaskListWithWarnings() (TaskListSchema.parse throws) and reddening the umbrellas-task-list-roundtrip guard. Pre-existing on HEAD; surfaced during S261 ledger promotions. Flipped 25.1-25.4 `cancelled` -> `deferred` (the only valid SubtaskStatus meaning set-aside/not-pursued). Task ID-25 itself remains `cancelled` — the authoritative cancellation signal lives on the parent Task, not the subtasks.
</info added on 2026-05-24T12:00:00.000Z>
